metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Family and Society 10 explores the interconnections between individuals, families, communities, and society through applied projects. Students examine relationships, child development, family structures, consumer decisions, personal finance, and social responsibility. They develop practical skills for healthy relationships, family management, and thoughtful citizenship while considering diverse perspectives and Indigenous approaches to family and community.
metadata.general_description: Elective course examining family dynamics, relationships, consumer skills, and social responsibility.

big_ideas.statement: Healthy families and relationships are built on communication, respect, responsibility, and mutual support.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students explore what makes relationships strong, how to navigate conflict, and how to support others. They learn that effective communication and emotional intelligence are skills that can be developed.

big_ideas.statement: Families take many forms and are shaped by culture, values, economic circumstances, and individual choices.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students examine diverse family structures and understand how culture, tradition, and personal values influence family life. They appreciate Indigenous perspectives on family and extended community.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding child development and parenting builds empathy and informs personal choices about future roles.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn how children grow and develop, what they need to thrive, and what parenting involves. This knowledge helps them understand their own development and consider future family decisions.

big_ideas.statement: Consumer and financial decisions reflect values and have personal, family, and community consequences.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop critical awareness of how their choices about money, purchases, and consumption affect their lives and the wider world. They learn to make thoughtful, values-aligned decisions.

competencies.raw: Design solutions for family and household challenges
i_can_statements: I can identify family and household problems that need solving
I can research and design solutions for daily living challenges
I can plan household projects that improve family life
I can evaluate how well solutions work for families

competencies.raw: Create communication and conflict resolution strategies
i_can_statements: I can design strategies for healthy family communication
I can develop approaches to manage and resolve conflict
I can create support systems for family members
I can design inclusive environments that respect all members

competencies.raw: Plan family and financial decisions thoughtfully
i_can_statements: I can create budgets for personal and family needs
I can design consumer plans aligned with values and needs
I can plan for major life decisions (education, housing, etc.)
I can consider long-term financial goals and strategies
I can identify community needs and develop response strategies
I can design projects that strengthen family-community connections
I can create initiatives that reflect diverse perspectives and values
I can contribute meaningfully to community improvement

competencies.raw: Build effective communication and relationship skills
i_can_statements: I can listen actively and respond empathetically
I can express myself clearly and assertively
I can recognize and manage emotions in myself and others
I can navigate disagreements respectfully
I can manage time and organize household responsibilities
I can plan and prepare meals for various needs
I can maintain a clean and organized living space
I can manage household supplies and resources

competencies.raw: Make informed consumer and financial decisions
i_can_statements: I can evaluate products and services critically
I can manage money responsibly and plan financially
I can understand credit, debt, and banking basics
I can compare options and make values-aligned choices
I can recognize developmental needs at different life stages
I can support others through transitions and challenges
I can advocate for healthy, respectful relationships
I can build and maintain meaningful connections

competencies.raw: Use technology to support family and household management
i_can_statements: I can use digital tools for scheduling and organization
I can leverage technology for household shopping and planning
I can use apps and software for financial management
I can find reliable online resources for family information

competencies.raw: Navigate digital communication and social media responsibly
i_can_statements: I can use digital tools for positive family communication
I can understand risks and benefits of social media
I can practice digital citizenship and respectful online behavior
I can protect privacy and security online

competencies.raw: Use technology for research and learning
i_can_statements: I can research family and social topics online critically
I can evaluate the credibility of online sources
I can find support resources and services online
I can access educational content about health and relationships

competencies.raw: Understand technology's impact on families and society
i_can_statements: I can analyze how technology affects family life and relationships
I can understand technology's role in social inequality
I can consider digital access and digital divides
I can use technology thoughtfully to enhance family connection

areas.name: Relationships and Communication
topics: Managing emotions and developing emotional intelligence

areas.name: Family Structures and Dynamics
topics: Indigenous perspectives on family and community

areas.name: Child Development and Care
topics: Child development from infancy through adolescence
Meeting children's physical, emotional, and developmental needs
Recognizing and supporting diverse developmental paths
Resources and support for families with children

areas.name: Consumer Skills and Financial Literacy
topics: Critical evaluation of advertising and marketing
